summary: Authority on tests for evaluating confessional statements.

chunks.heading: Issue
chunks.text: How should a court evaluate the truth of a confession?

chunks.heading: Holding
chunks.text: A confession can ground conviction if the court is satisfied it is true.

chunks.heading: Ratio
chunks.text: Courts should test a confession by examining whether it is direct, positive, and whether there are circumstances that make it probable or improbable.

summary: Authority on whether confession alone can sustain conviction.

chunks.heading: Issue
chunks.text: Whether a confession alone is sufficient to sustain a conviction.

chunks.heading: Holding
chunks.text: A free and voluntary confession alone may be sufficient to sustain a conviction.

chunks.heading: Ratio
chunks.text: A confession that is direct, positive and unequivocal, and accepted by the court as true, can ground a conviction without corroboration.

summary: Authority on retracted confessions and their evidential value.

chunks.heading: Issue
chunks.text: Whether a retracted confession can still be relied upon.

chunks.heading: Holding
chunks.text: A retracted confession may still ground conviction if the court is satisfied of its truth.

chunks.heading: Ratio
chunks.text: The court must test the truth of a retracted confession by examining surrounding circumstances and evidence before relying on it.

summary: Authority on personal liberty and unlawful detention.

chunks.heading: Issue
chunks.text: Whether detention without lawful justification violates constitutional rights.

chunks.heading: Holding
chunks.text: Unlawful detention constitutes a violation of the right to personal liberty.

chunks.heading: Ratio
chunks.text: Any detention not in accordance with procedure permitted by law violates Section 35 of the Constitution and entitles the victim to redress.

summary: Authority on bail discretion and national security considerations.

chunks.heading: Issue
chunks.text: Whether bail can be denied in the interest of national security.

chunks.heading: Holding
chunks.text: The court may deny bail where national security is threatened.

chunks.heading: Ratio
chunks.text: Where the interest of national security outweighs individual liberty, the court may refuse bail in the interest of justice.
